source_record.title: Multiple myeloma: signaling pathways and targeted therapy.
source_record.study_context: Molecular Biomedicine review discussing signaling pathways in multiple myeloma, including NF-kB pathway activation, bone marrow microenvironment interaction, proliferation, survival, migration, drug-resistance context, and limitations of approved pathway-targeted drugs. This public extraction uses the review only as second-source pathway-state navigation context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source describes NF-kB as one of the signaling pathways activated through myeloma cell and bone marrow microenvironment interaction, with aberrant activation linked to proliferation, survival, migration, angiogenesis, drug-resistance context, and multiple myeloma pathogenesis.
measurement_fields: canonical and non-canonical NF-kB pathway discussion
limitations: Review-level source; not a cohort-level MRD-positive selective plasma cell measurement.
Does not second-source the exact Cui 2024 posttreatment selective-cell state.
Supports pathway branch navigation only, not target selection, treatment recommendation, biomarker use, prognosis, monitoring, or clinical actionability.
Requires primary-source extraction before any claim upgrade.

what_this_supports: The nfkb-selective-state-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has one single-cell study signal and one review-level pathway context source.
The geometry should preserve NF-kB as a residual biological state branch while keeping the exact MRD-positive selective-cell signal source-specific.
NF-kB pathway context should stay descriptive and routed through contradiction and safety-invariance checks before reuse.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not recommend targeting NF-kB or any signaling pathway.
It does not rank NF-kB against other residual-state branches.
It does not interpret any patient MRD result or residual disease state.
It does not validate clinical actionability or cure claims.

review_notes: Created for Phase 4 productive-movement testing in mrd-resistance-geometry-proof-plan-v0.
Source details checked against the publisher page and PubMed-indexed metadata visible through search results.
The extraction intentionally weakens actionability language and uses the source as review-level navigation context only.
